cluster_label: Threading, snipping, drawing and dressing hands
product_name: Beads and Pattern Cards Activity Set
key_specs: {"piece_count":"108 hardwood beads, 20 pattern cards, 2 laces","dimensions":"Beads 2–2.5 cm; cards 11 x 29 cm","materials":"Hardwood beads, plastic-tipped laces","included_items":"108 beads, 20 double-sided activity cards, 2 x 92 cm laces, teaching notes","other":"LER0139"}
product_description_under_30_words: 108 colourful hardwood beads in spheres, cubes and cylinders, with 20 pattern cards and two long tipped laces for supervised threading and sequence copying.
ember_verdict: At nearly three, hands want a job, not just posting. The pattern cards turn lacing into copy-the-sequence play — the shift from grab-and-post to neater control. Beads are chunky hardwood, not jewellery size, but still 3+ small parts: sit together, keep cords short.
personalization_hint: likely to be copying simple colour or shape sequences and wanting a finished row to show you
why_it_fits: Only mainstream pick that directly supports copy-shapes threading at this stage; language-free cards suit independent table play.
caveats: Beads are the smaller end of chunky (about 2 cm). Long laces need adult supervision. Stock varies at UK specialists.
gift_note: Strong gift if parents want guided pattern play, not just loose beads.
ownership_note: Skip if you already own a plain bead crate — add pattern cards separately only if you will use them.
safety_notes: 3+ only. Small parts and long cords — supervise threading; store laces out of reach when not in use.
rating_source: Specialist retailer listing; brand spec verified
review_quality_note: Widely stocked in UK nurseries and OT suppliers; fewer mass-market Amazon reviews than Melissa & Doug.
evidence_sources: WordUnited product page | Learning Resources brand page | Primary ICT spec sheet
preloved_signal_note: Common in nursery clear-outs; check bead count and card condition.
substitute_if_unavailable: TTS Giant Threading Beads and Pattern Cards
evidence_notes: Bead size and 3+ warning confirmed on UK educational retailer pages.

cluster_label: Threading, snipping, drawing and dressing hands
key_specs: {"piece_count":"30 wooden beads, 2 laces, storage case","dimensions":"Pack approx. H34 x W29 x D9.5 cm","materials":"Wood, cord laces","included_items":"30 hand-painted beads in five shapes, two laces, wooden case","other":"SKU 7519288"}
product_description_under_30_words: Thirty chunky hand-painted wooden beads in five shapes, two long laces and a wooden storage case. Mainstream 3+ threading set for home play.
ember_verdict: The reliable baseline if you want one bead set and no faff. Beads are properly graspable — a step up from posting toys, without jumping to tiny craft beads. Borrow first if a crate already lives at grandparent's; buy new if this is your first supervised lacing kit.
personalization_hint: likely to be making colour lines, simple necklaces and wanting you to watch the finished string
why_it_fits: Widest UK availability, strong review proof, and bead size matches chunky 3+ brief.
caveats: Paint can chip with heavy chewing; storage case is flimsier than the beads.
gift_note: Easy wrap, recognised brand — safe default gift.
ownership_note: Very common duplicate; check toy box before buying.
safety_notes: Not suitable under 3. Choking hazard — supervise and keep away from younger siblings.
review_quality_note: Huge UK review base; themes praise grip size, flag occasional paint wear.
evidence_sources: Argos listing | Amazon UK reviews | John Lewis variant listing
preloved_signal_note: Abundant on Vinted/eBay; sanitise and check for chipped paint.
substitute_if_unavailable: Jaques of London Let's Play Threading Beads
evidence_notes: Age 3+ and choking warning on Argos and Amazon.

cluster_label: Threading, snipping, drawing and dressing hands
key_specs: {"piece_count":"90 beads, 6 tipped laces","dimensions":"Crate W13 x H12 x L13 cm","materials":"FSC-certified wood, child-safe paints","included_items":"90 multicolour beads, 6 aglet-tipped laces, wooden crate","other":"BJ662"}
product_description_under_30_words: Ninety colourful wooden beads with six tipped laces in a wooden storage crate. High piece count for pattern-making and shared table play.
ember_verdict: More beads, more repetition — useful when a nearly-three wants to build longer strings or a sibling joins in. Tipped laces reduce the fiddly end that derails focus. Wooden crate keeps pieces contained after supervised sessions.
personalization_hint: likely to be threading longer colour runs and returning to the activity across the week
why_it_fits: Best value piece-count for sustained lacing without tiny jewellery beads.
caveats: Beads are slightly smaller than Melissa & Doug primary beads; still 3+ supervised.
gift_note: Feels generous because of the crate and volume — good for shared households.
ownership_note: Overkill if you only need a small travel set.
safety_notes: 3+ small parts. Six laces mean extra cord supervision — shorten or remove when not playing.
review_quality_note: Solid UK volume; parents note tipped laces and storage crate.
evidence_sources: Bigjigs brand page | Amazon UK reviews | Trolley.co.uk price compare
preloved_signal_note: Wooden sets hold up well pre-loved.
evidence_notes: 3+ and European safety standards stated on brand page.

cluster_label: Threading, snipping, drawing and dressing hands
key_specs: {"piece_count":"30 wooden beads, laces, sliding-lid box","dimensions":"Box approx. 17 x 7.6 x 9.2 cm packaged","materials":"Solid wood, water-based paints","included_items":"Assorted shape beads, threading strings, wooden storage box","other":"30 beads, 6 colours"}
product_description_under_30_words: Thirty smooth wooden beads in soft colours with sturdy laces and a sliding-lid wooden box. Gift-ready UK wooden threading set for ages 3+.
ember_verdict: If the brief is a present that still earns its place, this beats a plastic jewellery tin. Chunky beads suit neater threading practice; the box makes it easy to bring back out on the kitchen table. Check bead holes on arrival — a few UK reviews mention rough interiors.
personalization_hint: likely to be sorting by colour first, then threading short runs with pride in the finished string
why_it_fits: Gift presentation plus genuinely usable chunky beads; matches 34–36m control shift.
caveats: Occasional rough internal holes; worth a quick sand if threading sticks.
gift_note: Ribboned Jaques packaging reads as intentional gift, not filler.
ownership_note: Functionally similar to Melissa & Doug — don't double up unless gifting.
safety_notes: 3+ small parts. Supervise; keep away from babies in the room.
review_quality_note: UK reviews praise presentation and bead size; note occasional rough holes.
evidence_sources: Jaques product page | Amazon UK reviews
preloved_signal_note: Less common than Melissa & Doug but box helps resale.
evidence_notes: 3+ warning on brand and Amazon listings.

cluster_label: Threading, snipping, drawing and dressing hands
key_specs: {"piece_count":"6 board pictures, 8 chunky laces","dimensions":"Pictures approx. 20 x 20 cm","materials":"Sturdy board, chunky plastic laces","included_items":"6 animal lacing boards, 8 coloured laces, play guide","other":"BH-100"}
product_description_under_30_words: Six sturdy animal board pictures with pre-cut holes and eight chunky coloured laces. Flat 3+ lacing activity for table or travel.
ember_verdict: Not every child wants loose beads first. These boards give the same neat-hand challenge — follow holes, pull the lace through — without a carpet full of rollers. Ideal alongside a bead set, or on its own for café/table play.
personalization_hint: likely to be finishing a whole picture and wanting to show the laced animal
why_it_fits: Chunky lacing with zero tiny beads; supports neater control and supervised 3+ play.
caveats: Laces are consumable — they tangle and wear; not a necklace-making set.
gift_note: Budget-friendly add-on gift or stocking filler that still fits the brief.
ownership_note: Different format from bead crates — complements rather than duplicates.
safety_notes: Not suitable under 36 months. Supervise laces; keep away from younger siblings.
review_quality_note: Long-running Galt SKU; fewer aggregated ratings than bead sets.
evidence_sources: Galt Toys UK product page | Austins UK retailer listing
evidence_notes: 3 years+ and under-36-months warning on Galt page.
